title: Aeschynanthus breviflorus
taxonomy: Aeschynanthus breviflorus is classified in the family Gesneriaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the gesneriad family. This family includes over 3,000 species, many of which are native to tropical regions of Central and South America, as well as parts of Asia. Aeschynanthus breviflorus is a member of the genus Aeschynanthus, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in Southeast Asia. The species is further classified into its own taxonomic group due to its unique characteristics and growth habits. Within the Aeschynanthus genus, Aeschynanthus breviflorus is often distinguished by its distinctive red flowers and compact growth habit.
uses: Aeschynanthus breviflorus, commonly known as the lipstick plant, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which could be useful in treating various infections and inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits. The plant's antioxidant properties may also contribute to its potential health benefits.

constituents: Aeschynanthus breviflorus contains various active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic compounds. preparations: Aeschynanthus breviflorus, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
